subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the setting and context of Buddha’s sermon at Benares
Identify the main ideas and teachings presented in the sermon
Recognize key terms such as Dharma, the Middle Path, and Four Noble Truths
Develop reading comprehension and summarization skills
Appreciate the relevance of Buddha’s teachings in daily life

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Buddha and Context
topics_covered: Who Buddha was and the historical context
Setting: Benares and the audience of the sermon
Purpose of the sermon and its importance
teaching_strategy: Storytelling approach with visual aids showing Benares and Buddha
Explain simple background of Buddha’s life and enlightenment
student_activities: Discuss why Buddha wanted to teach people about life and suffering
assessment: Oral Q&A on Buddha and the setting of the sermon
Short written note on the purpose of the sermon

lesson_plan.title: Buddha’s Main Teachings – Part 1
topics_covered: Introduction to the Four Noble Truths
teaching_strategy: Use simple stories or examples to explain suffering
Explain the Four Noble Truths in simple language
student_activities: List the Four Noble Truths in own words
Discuss examples of suffering in daily life
assessment: Oral Q&A on the Four Noble Truths
Short written note summarizing what they understood about suffering

lesson_plan.title: Buddha’s Main Teachings – Part 2
topics_covered: The Middle Path and its meaning
teaching_strategy: Explain Middle Path with examples of moderation
Discuss simple ways to apply teachings in daily life
student_activities: Give examples of situations where the Middle Path can be applied
Discuss how following Dharma helps in everyday life
assessment: Oral Q&A on Middle Path and practical application
Short written note on one way to follow Buddha’s teachings

lesson_plan.title: Significance of the Sermon
topics_covered: Impact of the sermon on the first followers
Importance of Dharma and moral conduct
teaching_strategy: Discuss significance using simple examples and storytelling
Use visuals showing Buddha teaching the people
student_activities: Discuss in pairs how the sermon influenced people
Write one sentence on why the sermon is important today
assessment: Oral discussion on significance and relevance
Short written reflection connecting lessons to daily life

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Reflection
topics_covered: Summary of key teachings: Four Noble Truths, Middle Path
teaching_strategy: Use timeline or flashcards for revision
Encourage students to share insights and understanding
student_activities: Create a simple chart of Four Noble Truths and Middle Path
Write a few lines on how they can follow Buddha’s teachings in life
assessment: Oral quiz on key teachings and significance
Class discussion on reflections and moral lessons

teaching_aids: Images of Buddha delivering the sermon
Flashcards with key teachings and terms
Timeline or chart summarizing main teachings
teacher_tip: Use storytelling and visuals to help students understand Buddha’s teachings. Focus on key ideas such as suffering, Four Noble Truths, and the Middle Path. Encourage students to reflect on how these teachings are relevant to daily life.
